title: BUG: Failed `!PyErr_Occurred()` assertion
author: devdanzin
body:
### Describe the issue:

It's possible to abort a debug build by calling some methods with invalid values like in the code example. Only reproductions using `collections.Counter` were identified, but I can search for other inputs than can also cause this abort if it would help.

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
import numpy
from collections import Counter
numpy.promote_types(Counter(), 1.1)

# or

numpy.clongdouble().getfield(Counter())

# or

numpy.long().sum(3655, Counter(), bytes(10 ** 5))
```

### Error message:

```shell
python: Objects/descrobject.c:268: int method_check_args(PyObject *, PyObject *const *, Py_ssize_t, PyObject *): Assertion `!PyErr_Occurred()' failed.
```

### Python and NumPy Versions:

2.3.0.dev0+git20250415.e151f0d
3.13.3+ experimental free-threading build (heads/3.13:83cb89b941b, Apr 18 2025, 20:59:43) [Clang 19.1.7 (++20250114103253+cd708029e0b2-1~exp1~20250114103309.40)]

 {'architecture': 'SkylakeX',
  'filepath': '/home/danzin/venvs/3.13_upstream_fusil_venv/lib/python3.13t/site-packages/numpy.libs/libscipy_openblas64_-56d6093b.so',
  'internal_api': 'openblas',
  'num_threads': 16,
  'prefix': 'libscipy_openblas',
  'threading_layer': 'pthreads',
  'user_api': 'blas',
  'version': '0.3.29'}]

### Context for the issue:

I have been fuzzing Numpy using [fusil](https://github.com/devdanzin/fusil) by @vstinner. I realize these crashes are unlikely to be triggered in normal usage and therefore might be of low priority.

The fuzzing was done with an ASAN free-threading clang build and not confirmed on a GILfull non-sanitizer GCC build yet.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body:
This seems to be the same issue, but the backtrace looks a bit different so here it is for the record:
```python
import numpy._core
f = numpy._core.float32()
f.put([[]], (), -2**31)
```

comments.author: tylerjereddy
comments.body: Just to note, I believe @seberg recently asked for static analyzer-based bug detection reports to be condensed to a single issue where possible. I guess your tickets are already open, but I suspect the motivation was just to separate real-world user reports from the "automatic" reports and perhaps to prevent the latter from drowning out the former to some extent.

comments.author: devdanzin
comments.body: Thanks for the heads up. I'd be OK with closing these except by one and condensing there, if it's considered more appropriate.

comments.author: seberg
comments.body:
There has been a wash of very low impact but verbose issues without often without any context (how was it found/what does it impact; and not just in NumPy).  So these are better, if also low impact mostly.

There are at least 2 different issues here. FWIW, for the `put` case (but likely also for the others), it may be best to skip scalars and trip with `np.asarray(f)...` directly.

For:
```
import numpy._core
f = numpy._core.float32()
f.put([[]], (), -2**31)
``` 
the error in a non-debug version is much clearer, because python will still detect this, but also chain the original error.

That is an easy fix (the other likely also), seems like some parsing paths are a hot mess when it comes to errors (and test coverage was not added properly when doing this).
